original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Faecal incontinence in adults: management — Recommendations
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults reporting faecal incontinence; neurological, obstetric, postoperative, prolapse, cancer-warning, and acute spinal contexts need tailored assessment.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Supports physical, emotional, social, bowel, medical, cognitive, and clinician-performed anorectal assessment documentation without defaulting an intimate examination or generating treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.3 — impact and baseline assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports relevant medical history, contributory factors, physical and emotional impact, general examination, anorectal examination only if performed, cognition, and warning context.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.6.1 — review and specialist context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-entered symptom review, prior management, specialist assessment status, preferences, dignity, and follow-up documentation.

title: Faecal incontinence in adults: management — Recommendations
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
population: Adults reporting faecal incontinence; neurological, obstetric, postoperative, prolapse, cancer-warning, and acute spinal contexts need tailored assessment.
intended_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
